cases.title: Audit the full package surface with native tooling
cases.user_request: Run a dependency and package-surface security audit for this repo and summarize the real findings.

notes: A package manifest and lockfile are present.
The report should stay focused on dependency and package-surface risk.
available_artifacts: npm audit or equivalent ecosystem-native tooling

review_questions: Does the report stay grounded in dependency or package-surface evidence rather than drifting into general code review?
Does it explain what was audited and why that uniting makes sense for the repo?
Are findings ranked with enough detail for a follow-on remediation decision?

cases.title: Selected package scope with explicit boundaries
cases.user_request: Only audit the root Node package for dependency security and tell me what matters most.

notes: The audit should stay on the selected package surface only.

static_checks: Mentions what was intentionally left out of scope.
review_questions: Does the report stay within the selected package surface?
Does it avoid broad claims about services or packages that were not audited?
Would the user understand the most important dependency risks for the chosen scope?

cases.title: Missing best-in-class tooling should be reported as a gap
cases.user_request: Audit this repo for dependency vulnerabilities and license issues, but tell me plainly if the best scanner is unavailable.

notes: Some preferred scanners may be unavailable locally.
The response should still use the strongest available checks.

static_checks: States what coverage was unavailable because tooling was missing.
review_questions: Does the report use the strongest available checks without pretending missing tooling does not matter?
Does it clearly state what coverage remains incomplete?
Does it avoid manufacturing findings from tools that were never run?
